rows.text:
A jury found Francisco Frias-Abalos, of Mexico, guilty of indecent assault. The court sentenced him to 200 days confinement, but his sentence was suspended. He ended up on probation and ordered to have no contact with a specific woman, who soon after filed a protective order against him.

ICE @ERODallas Fugitive Operations arrested him at-large during a targeted immigration enforcement action. 

He’s now in ICE detention and going through removal proceedings based on:

⚖️ INA 212(a)(6)(A): Alien present without admission or parole
rows.text_resolved:

rows.text:
Earlier this month, @USDOL reinforced commonsense English language proficiency requirements for commercial drivers to help ensure they are properly trained and prepared.

Today’s tragedy is a heartbreaking reminder why.

If you can’t understand the rules of the road or read road signs, you shouldn’t be behind the wheel.

My prayers are with the victims, their families, and everyone impacted by this devastating loss.

ICE has issued a detainer against Rodrigo Antonio Rodriguez Flores, of Mexico, after he was charged with DUI and vehicular homicide in Gwinnett County, Georgia.
 
⚖️A 2024 Georgia state law prohibits all local governments from creating sanctuary policies. In other words, Gwinnett County honors ICE detainers, so Rodriguez Flores will not be released into the community before he faces justice.

A jury found Francisco Frias-Abalos, of Mexico, guilty of indecent assault. The court sentenced him to 200 days confinement, but his sentence was suspended. He ended up on probation and ordered to have no contact with a specific woman, who soon after filed a protective order against him. ICE @ERODallas Fugitive Operations arrested him at-large during a targeted immigration enforcement action. ICE has issued a detainer against Rodrigo Antonio Rodriguez Flores, of Mexico, after he was charged with DUI and vehicular homicide in Gwinnett County, Georgia. ⚖️A 2024 Georgia state law prohibits all local governments from creating sanctuary policies. In other words, Gwinnett County honors ICE detainers, so Rodriguez Flores will not be released into the community before he faces justice.
